methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
Ambient.ai is a Series B-stage AI & Software company that has raised $146M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, World Labs at $5.0B, Nominal at $1.0B, and Photoroom at $500M), supplemented by one public comparable in the space.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $146M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $277M. Peer-median analysis derives an implied value of $5.0B based on the median valuation across the five same-stage AI & Software comparables, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion ($500M floor to $32.0B ceiling).

The two methods produce materially divergent signals. Reconciling them yields a triangulated range of $236M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.6B. The wide band reflects genuine uncertainty around where Ambient.ai sits within the current AI & Software funding environment—specifically, whether the company's commercial traction and technology defensibility align it with lower-valuation peers like Nominal and Photoroom or the higher-valuation cohort. Resolution of this range depends on clarity around revenue metrics, customer concentration, and product differentiation relative to the peer set.
